//: FULCRUM profile config. Pass with `fulcrum rank ... --config this.json`.
//progress_point: The throughput marker you call probe::progress(name) with. Its visit-rate is the program's 'work done' signal.
//regions: The candidate optimization targets. `name` MUST match what you pass to probe::scope(name). `source` ranges fold Coz line-experiments into the region (match by file basename + line range). `functions` are substrings used to (a) attribute perf-report cycles for the mechanism and (b) map critical-path span labels back to the region.
//ground_truth: Optional self-checks (see `fulcrum validate`). The Coz-based checks (known_non_lever / known_levers) need a profile.coz. The cp_* checks run from the trace alone.
//consumer: Drives `fulcrum consumer` (WAIT/COMPUTE/OUTPUT/IDLE split of the in-order consumer's wall). thread_prefix identifies the consumer thread. Each class is a matcher {exact,prefixes,suffixes,substrings} (OR-combined). WAIT also always recognizes the universal convention (wait.* / *.wait / *recv*), so a wait matcher is only needed for non-conventional names. idle_umbrellas are the outer-loop wrappers whose own self-time is the un-instrumented inter-child gap.
//stages: Drives `fulcrum flow` (per-stage wall-critical vs slack). Matched in declaration order; FIRST match wins (declare catch-alls last). A name starting with '·' is a recognized non-stage (a wait or umbrella): kept out of UNCLASSIFIED but contributing no busy work.
//inner_blockers: Worker inner-phase span names to prefer when several overlap a consumer wait, so the stall is blamed on the real phase, not the task umbrella that wraps it.
